SDK_ABSTRACTION_REQUIRED.description: SDK must abstract LiveKit into a simple consumer API
rules: Consumers must not interact with LiveKit directly
SDK must expose unified callbacks
SDK must handle initialization internally
SDK must unify connection, signaling, and lifecycle

ARCH_VOICE_CALL_SDK_FACADE_V1.description: VoiceCallSdk.liveKit is the primary facade for sdk_connect voice calls and owns CallEngine plus LiveKit media lifecycle
rules: VoiceCallSdk.liveKit manages CallEngine creation and disposal
VoiceCallSdk.liveKit manages LiveKit media engine lifecycle internally
Example app and README should use VoiceCallSdk as the primary integration path

ARCH_CALL_ENGINE_SSOT_V2.description: CallEngine remains the single source of truth even when wrapped by VoiceCallSdk
rules: Call state mutations flow through CallEngine
Facade layers must not duplicate call lifecycle state
Unified callbacks may observe state but not replace engine authority

SEC_SIGNAL_VALIDATION_REQUIRED_V1.description: Signal validation is mandatory before VoiceCallSdk processes call setup
rules: VoiceCallSdk must reject invalid or incomplete signaling payloads
Token and signaling validation remain required preconditions for call setup

SEC_SECURE_RANDOM_CALL_ID_V1.description: Call identifiers must use secure random generation rather than timestamps
rules: Timestamp-based call IDs are not permitted
Generated call IDs must be suitable for external signaling contexts

TEST_WIDGET_SKIP_USER_APPROVED_V1.description: A known flaky widget test may remain skipped when explicitly approved by the user and the rest of the suite passes
rules: Document the intentional skip in memory or task notes
Keep the skip scoped to the flaky test only
Preserve passing coverage for the remaining suite

ARCH_SDKCONNECT_PUBLIC_FACADE_V1.description: SDKConnect is the neutral public facade and documented entry point for sdk_connect
rules: SDKConnect is the primary and documented public entry point
Barrel exports are narrowed to SDKConnect facade and core models
Callbacks and events are unified under SDKConnect
Voice flow uses callType=voice and reserves video for additive expansion
Example app and README use SDKConnect.create as the integration path
VoiceCallSdk remains an internal compatibility layer behind SDKConnect

ARCH_VIDEO_CALL_ADDITIVE_V1.description: Video support added additively via SDKConnectCallType.video; no breaking changes to voice path
rules: startCall/acceptCall auto-enable camera when callType=video via CallEngine.setVideoEnabled
VoiceCallSdk.setVideoEnabled and toggleCamera are wired through to CallEngine
Voice callType remains unaffected by video additions

MEDIA_VIDEO_CAMERA_V1.description: MediaEngine extended with camera control; CallState tracks video enablement
rules: MediaEngine interface exposes setCameraOn(bool) and isVideoEnabled
LiveKitMediaEngine implements setCameraOn via localParticipant.setCameraEnabled
CallState includes isVideoEnabled field as source of truth for video state

SEC_TOKEN_EXPIRY_CHECK_V1.description: JWT expiry enforced in CallEngine before media connect
rules: CallEngine._validateConnectParams decodes JWT payload via base64url
exp claim is checked against current clock time
Expired tokens throw CallLifecycleException before any media connection attempt

SEC_SIGNAL_REJECT_EMITS_ERROR_V1.description: Invalid or rejected signals emit VoiceCallErrorEvent instead of silent drop
rules: VoiceCallSdk._handleSignal emits VoiceCallErrorEvent on invalid envelope (signal.validation_failed)
signalValidator rejection emits VoiceCallErrorEvent (signal.validation_rejected)
Silent signal drops are not permitted
New tests cover: video start enables camera, setVideoEnabled toggle, expired JWT rejection, malformed JWT, signalValidator rejection emits error, invalid envelope emits error
Widget test skip remains intentional per TEST_WIDGET_SKIP_USER_APPROVED_V1
